site.name: Octopus
site.description: One task, one agent, direct delivery. Octopus moves AI from chat windows into real workflows. Free, open source, and 100% local.

home.title: Task lists do not ship themselves
home.subtitle: Octopus turns AI chat into a task-driven delivery pipeline. Describe what needs to be shipped and each agent carries the right context to research, draft, execute, and return results. Free, open source, and 100% local.

foundation.title: Not a chat assistant, but a deliverable agent system
foundation.body: The core idea is not more chat tabs. Octopus puts tasks, context, execution, approvals, and artifacts into one operating flow.

consulting.title: Ready to move agents into real business workflows?
consulting.body: We help teams plan deployment, data boundaries, governance, and workflow integration before production rollout.

taskAgent.title: 1 task = 1 agent
taskAgent.desc: Every task gets a dedicated agent with a clear goal, isolated context, and a traceable execution path.

local.title: 100% local and private
local.desc: Run everything on your machine or private environment with zero telemetry and no mandatory cloud dependency.

parallel.title: 15+ parallel agents
parallel.desc: Move more than 15 tasks forward at once, then pause, resume, and continue without losing context.

context.title: 4-layer deep context engine
context.desc: Organization knowledge, project context, team standards, and task instructions all flow into execution together.

automation.title: Scheduling and automation
automation.desc: Recurring work can run on daily, weekly, or custom schedules so agents complete the full loop automatically.

visibility.title: Live visibility and approval gates
visibility.desc: Every action, progress signal, artifact, and approval point stays visible for real team collaboration and governance.

workflow.title: Task in, result out
workflow.body: Write a task, not a prompt. Create, dispatch, and deliver in three steps.

steps.title: Create
steps.heading: Write a task, not a prompt
steps.desc: Describe what needs to be delivered and Octopus attaches project files, past work, and linked tasks to define the expected artifact.

steps.title: Dispatch
steps.heading: One task -> one agent
steps.desc: Each task gets its own autonomous agent with organization knowledge, team standards, and project context so many tasks can run in parallel.

steps.title: Deliver
steps.heading: Review, approve, finish
steps.desc: Agents research, draft, build, and publish while keeping the process visible, the result reviewable, and the deliverables captured.

comparison.title: Chat windows were not built for real work
comparison.body: Ephemeral conversations do not carry repeatable operations well. Octopus turns AI into a durable task system.

items.chat: Constant copy-paste between AI chat and actual work
items.octopus: Every agent is bound to one task, so the goal and reason stay explicit

items.chat: All context disappears in the next conversation
items.octopus: Full context stays persistent, so you can pause, resume, and recover any task

items.chat: Outputs get buried in chat history and become hard to find later
items.octopus: Deliverables are captured automatically, searchable, and easy to revisit

items.chat: No way to track what AI is doing across the team
items.octopus: A live dashboard keeps every agent's progress visible

items.chat: No reliable way to automate repeated AI work
items.octopus: Recurring tasks can be scheduled and executed by agents automatically

items.chat: Little visibility into quality or progress
items.octopus: Progress, quality, and approval points all remain transparent

useCases.title: One task, one agent, for any team
useCases.body: From market intelligence to feature delivery and finance reporting, once a task is defined clearly it can be handed to the right agent.

items.title: Competitive intelligence tracking
items.task: Track competitor changelogs, pricing shifts, and feature launches every week.

items.title: Parallel feature delivery
items.task: Read the spec and code context, then deliver components, APIs, tests, and a PR summary in parallel.

items.title: Board-level reporting
items.task: Pull revenue, burn, and runway data to generate a monthly board report with an executive summary.

items.question: Who is Octopus for?
items.answer: Any team with real work to ship. Marketing, sales, finance, HR, support, design, and engineering can all express work as tasks and let agents take it forward.

items.question: What is task-driven AI?
items.answer: Each work unit starts as a task and gets its own autonomous agent. Context stays isolated, execution stays durable, and results become artifacts instead of disappearing into chat.

items.question: What kinds of tasks can agents handle?
items.answer: Web research, content writing, data analysis, code generation, reporting, competitive intelligence, and design QA can all be completed end to end as tasks.

items.question: How is this different from ChatGPT-style tools?
items.answer: Chat tools optimize for one-off conversations. Octopus optimizes for ongoing delivery with task context, execution traces, approval points, and captured outputs.

items.question: Can agents be paused and resumed?
items.answer: Yes. Tasks, conversations, files, and progress are preserved so work can continue hours or days later.

items.question: Is it free?
items.answer: Yes. Octopus is fully free and Apache 2.0 open source, without a hosted subscription requirement.

items.question: Does data get uploaded to the cloud?
items.answer: No. Octopus supports fully local operation and keeps data inside your device or private environment by default.

product.title: Task-Driven Agent OS
product.body: Chat windows work for Q&A, not for delivery. Octopus gives every task a persistent agent with full context, live progress, and captured artifacts so AI can move from talking to shipping.

narrative.title: Not a chat box, but an operating system for delivery
narrative.body: Tasks, context, tools, approvals, artifacts, and governance live in one desktop workbench so agents can keep operating instead of restarting from zero each time.

modules.title: Keep task orchestration, capabilities, and governance in one surface
modules.body: These four modules make up the Octopus operating model.

items.title: Task-to-agent pipeline
items.desc: The built-in tracker turns every task into an autonomous agent, with subtasks that can keep splitting into their own contextual work units.

items.title: Capabilities center
items.desc: Package organization knowledge, standards, skills, and integrations into agents so they work by your rules instead of improvising from scratch.

items.title: Command center
items.desc: Watch every agent's task, progress, action trail, and approval status from one screen instead of piecing updates together by hand.

items.title: IM and automation entry points
items.desc: Create tasks through Telegram, Discord, Feishu, WeChat, or schedulers so repeated work keeps running and exceptions surface immediately.

items.title: Execution boundaries stay explicit
items.desc: From tool calls to task execution, every action is constrained by clear operating limits so digital employees can act without overreaching.

items.title: Operational context stays replayable
items.desc: Every collaboration chain, invocation record, and output artifact can be inspected, traced, and reviewed after the fact.

items.title: Governance policy lands in the system
items.desc: Roles, permissions, and audit requirements are enforced at the platform layer instead of remaining presentation-only concepts.

mcp.title: Give agents your real tools
mcp.desc: Connect databases, internal systems, and APIs through standard protocols so agents can do real work instead of only answering.

sandbox.title: Autonomous research and execution
sandbox.desc: Built-in browsing and isolated execution let agents research the web, extract information, build charts, and act inside controlled boundaries.

telemetry.title: Visible process, replayable results
telemetry.desc: From reasoning flow to tool calls and final artifacts, every critical step stays observable, reviewable, and replayable.

plugin.title: Turn team standards into skills
plugin.desc: Package SOPs, role responsibilities, and domain workflows into reusable skills and plugins that expand each agent role.

desktop.title: All tasks on one desktop workbench
desktop.desc: Keep conversations, tasks, knowledge, resources, and artifacts on one desktop surface so work can pause and resume without losing state.

enterprise.title: Permissions, approvals, and governance together
enterprise.desc: Move roles, permissions, approvals, and audit requirements into the system layer instead of leaving them as presentation-only ideas.

scenarios.title: What can it do for you?
scenarios.body: These are not demo agents. They are task-oriented agents built to keep working across individual, team, and enterprise environments.

segments.title: Individual: your always-on digital assistant
segments.desc: Keep long-running context on the desktop for research, writing, coding, and document work instead of re-explaining your background every time.

segments.title: Team: a shared pool of digital employees
segments.desc: Dispatch agents for copywriting, research, project follow-up, and code review so AI teammates can work in parallel with human contributors.

segments.title: Enterprise: private, governable, auditable
segments.desc: Fit organizations with deployment boundaries and compliance requirements by pushing permissions, auditability, and data safety into the workflow itself.

useCases.title: Real task lists across teams
useCases.body: Each example follows the same pattern: write the outcome as a task, then let the agent carry the context and finish the work.

items.title: Competitive intelligence tracking
items.task: Track competitor changelogs, pricing shifts, and feature launches every week.
workflow: Trigger automatically every Monday morning
Collect competitor site updates and changelogs
Generate a comparison report with highlighted changes
Post the report to the strategy channel

items.title: Launch autopilot
items.task: Prepare a Product Hunt launch with competitor analysis, landing page copy, and social assets.
workflow: Research the top competing products
Draft positioning, messaging, and landing page copy
Generate social posts for each channel
Build a launch-day publishing schedule

items.title: Account intelligence analysis
items.task: Research target companies and decision-makers, then draft personalized outreach emails.
workflow: Collect company profiles and funding updates
Analyze tech stack, growth signals, and contacts
Generate personalized outreach for each account
Push account cards into the sales channel

items.title: Automated design QA
items.task: Compare production UI against design files, mark regressions, and suggest fixes.
workflow: Capture screenshots from core pages
Compare them against the design source
Turn visual diffs into annotated subtasks
Generate pixel-level repair guidance

items.title: Parallel feature delivery
items.task: Build a profile page with avatar upload, bio editing, and activity feed support.
workflow: Read the spec and code context
Implement components, routes, and data model changes
Add unit and integration tests
Open a pull request with a complete diff summary

items.title: Board-level reporting
items.task: Generate a monthly board report with revenue trends, burn rate, and runway forecasts.
workflow: Pull data from finance systems
Produce charts and variance analysis
Format the report with an executive summary
Distribute it with highlighted metrics

about.title: About Octopus
about.body: Octopus aims to move AI from one-off conversation tools into a local agent system that can actually deliver work.

story.title: What we are building
story.lead: Every team has work to deliver, but chat-based AI usually stops at a single answer. Octopus connects tasks, context, execution, and governance into one operating system that can keep running.
story.detail: That is why we chose a task-driven, fully local, open, and extensible path so individuals, teams, and enterprises can run controllable agents inside their own environments.

principles.title: Our design principles
principles.body: These principles explain why Octopus is built the way it is.

items.title: Tasks first, not chats first
items.desc: Every unit of work should have a clear goal, context, and deliverable instead of being scattered through chat history.

items.title: Local first, data stays in-bounds
items.desc: Privacy and control are not optional extras. Data should stay on your machine or inside your private environment by default.

items.title: Open and extensible
items.desc: Skills, plugins, and standard protocols should connect agents to real toolchains instead of locking users into a closed box.

items.title: Governance before scale
items.desc: Permissions, approvals, auditability, and replay have to work first before agents belong in real business operations.

bookDemo.title: Bring agents into real business workflows
bookDemo.body: Share your organization and requirements. We will respond with advice grounded in deployment choices, data boundaries, governance controls, and workflow integration.

valueProps.title: What the consultation covers
valueProps.body: We focus on the points that usually block the move from trial to production.

items.title: Deployment path and environment choice
items.desc: Evaluate local, intranet, private cloud, or hybrid options and define the rollout path.

items.title: Data boundaries and security policy
items.desc: Map sensitive-data handling, secret management, logging scope, and permission isolation requirements.

items.title: Workflow and system integration
items.desc: Identify the knowledge bases, databases, IM channels, approval chains, and internal systems that need to connect.

items.title: Task design and governance rules
items.desc: Turn roles, approvals, auditing, and exception handling into an executable agent workflow.

bookDemo.successBody: Thanks for reaching out. Our team will contact you within two business days to confirm your scenario, deployment approach, and governance requirements.

experience.title: One desktop workbench for agent collaboration
experience.desc: The desktop client keeps conversations, tools, knowledge, and governance in one operational surface so digital employees can keep working across real business flows instead of isolated demos.
